profile_basis: Post-pass112 RCH fj-dispatch vmap profile on ts2: vmap_eigh/batched_matrix_3x3 161.90us, vmap_svd/batched_matrix_3x2 110.91us, vmap_qr/batched_matrix_3x2 88.411us, vmap_while/functional_sub_jaxpr_sub_two_128 88.011us, vmap_dot_i64/paired_vectors 79.139us. frankenjax-lr0pf was the ready [perf] linalg bead covering register-blocked QR/SVD and small-matrix linalg.
lever: Unrolled bit-identical 3x2 thin Householder QR helper inside fj-dispatch batching for the exact m=3, n=2, full_matrices=false benchmark shape. All nonmatching shapes, modes, and element types fall through to the existing generic QR batching path.
baseline_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-baseline cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- vmap_qr/batched_matrix_3x2 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
rebench_command: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-rebench cargo bench -p fj-dispatch --bench dispatch_baseline -- vmap_qr/batched_matrix_3x2 --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1 --noplot
isomorphism_proof: The fast path is gated to m == 3, n == 2, and full_matrices == false inside the existing batch_qr_multi path. All other QR modes and shapes continue to call qr_decompose_matrix exactly as before.
Accepted inputs are read in the same row-major order from the same tensor element slice. Nonnumeric elements still use Literal::as_f64 and return the same type-mismatch EvalError text.

The unrolled helper preserves the generic Householder operation order for the 3x2 thin case: column-0 reflector, column-1 reflector, reverse reflector application to Q, then diagonal sign normalization.

Floating-point threshold checks, dot-product term order, subtraction order, diagonal sign checks, and signed-zero-producing negations match the generic helper. The focused unit test compares Q and R f64::to_bits output against qr_decompose_matrix(3, 2, matrix, false).
There is no RNG, no side effect, and no comparison tie-breaking in the accepted QR fragment. Batch slice ordering and Q/R output extension order are unchanged.
validation: RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-tests cargo test -p fj-dispatch qr_ --lib -- --nocapture
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-check cargo check -p fj-dispatch --all-targets
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-libtests cargo test -p fj-dispatch --lib
RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch-frankenjax-lr0pf-qr-clippy cargo clippy -p fj-dispatch --all-targets -- -D warnings
